topic: Improve the sk-code code-quality sub-skill and shared assets/references; make code-quality more useful and better integrated into repo systems including spec-kit, skill-advisor, deep-loops, benchmarks, hooks, and general coding standards; ground findings in the current .opencode codebase

normalized_topic: improve the sk-code code-quality sub-skill and shared assets references make code-quality more useful and better integrated into repo systems including spec-kit skill-advisor deep-loops benchmarks hooks and general coding standards ground findings in the current opencode codebase

focus: Baseline current code-quality sub-skill and sk-code/shared resource layout
noveltyJustification: 6 of 6 findings were new baseline facts for this fresh packet, including contract, routing, shared-reference, checklist-path, script-enforcement, and advisor-metadata observations.
keyQuestions: What does .opencode/skills/sk-code/code-quality/ currently provide, and where is it useful, redundant, missing, or friction-heavy?
What shared sk-code assets/references does code-quality rely on, and which shared contracts should be improved for all sk-code modes?
answeredQuestions: Current code-quality workflow and boundaries are baselined.
Current shared sk-code dependencies and immediate navigation/pathing gaps are baselined.
ruledOut: Adding or expecting code-quality/graph-metadata.json; the parent hub owns the single advisor identity.
Treating code-quality as findings-first review; code-review owns that output.

focus: System-spec-kit integration for code-quality validation, completion, memory continuity, and dist-freshness handoffs

answeredQuestions: code-quality should load/reference system-spec-kit validation, completion, continuity, and dist-freshness contracts as owned handoffs rather than duplicating them.
ruledOut: Duplicating system-spec-kit validation or memory-save rules inside code-quality.

focus: System-skill-advisor integration for code-quality parent metadata, quality-mode aliases, benchmark vocabulary, prompt-safe recommendation evidence, and single sk-code advisor identity

answeredQuestions: code-quality should be surfaced through parent-level sk-code metadata plus quality-mode aliases; benchmarks should assert expectedSkill sk-code and hub workflowMode quality without creating a separate advisor target.

Surfacing raw prompt snippets as advisor recommendation evidence.

focus: Deep-loop, hook, behavior-benchmark, and general software-quality integration for code-quality evidence handoff

keyQuestions: How should code-quality integrate with deep-loop research/review flows, hooks, behavior benchmarks, and general software-quality standards?
answeredQuestions: code-quality should emit durable author-side quality evidence that deep-review/deep-research can consume; hook warnings are triage only; behavior benchmarks should cover comment-hygiene hook and deep-review handoff paths.

Treating PostToolUse hook warnings as blocking quality-pass evidence.

focus: Unresolved workflow and artifact-contract gaps for code-quality integration: route-proof expectations, JSONL/stable-key evidence, delta/artifact gaps, completion-claim verification, and downstream-consumable quality-gate evidence.
